etymology.description: semantic 糸(糸) (mì, "silk; thread") + phonetic 罔(罔) (wǎng) -> net
etymology.interpretation: The character 網(wǎng, "net") combines the semantic component 糸(糸) (mì, "silk; thread"), which suggests the material or structure of a net, with the phonetic component 罔(罔) (wǎng), which provides the sound. This combination depicts a net woven from thread, emphasizing its woven and entangling nature.
etymology.simplification: The simplified form 网 replaces 糸(糸) with a simplified representation of the net structure, reducing the complexity of the character while maintaining the essential depiction of a net.

examples.chinese: 我们用网捕捉蝴蝶(我們用網捕捉蝴蝶)。
examples.english: We use a net to catch butterflies.

examples.chinese: 渔夫撒网捕鱼(漁夫撒網捕魚)。
examples.english: The fisherman casts a net to catch fish.

examples.chinese: 大网在海中展开(大網在海中展開)，捕捉了许多鱼(捕捉了許多魚)。
examples.pinyin: Dà wǎng zài hǎi zhōng zhǎnkāi, bǔzhuōle xǔduō yú.
examples.english: The large net spread in the sea, capturing many fish.
